rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Deft For Requests For Admissions-Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 26, DEFENDANT JULIA VARGAS Ntc Of Motion To Compel Further & Proper Responses To Deft For Requests For Admissions The court adopts the recommendation of Judge Pro Tem Renne as follows: Granted. Plaintiffs shall seve full and complete responses, without objection, no later than February 1, 2013. Failure to do so will result in the requests being deemed admitted, and Plaintiffs will be precluded from introducing contradictory evidence. Monetary sanctions of $ 1,186.00 are awarded in favor of Ms. Vargas and against Plaintiff and their counsel to be paid no later than February 15, 2013. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: C & J LEASING INC et al VS. MOHSEN HASSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Temporary Restraining Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 1, Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause To Defendant Re: Temporary Restraining Order Denied. By order dated December 24, 2012, granting Ex Parte Order Shortening Time Re Motion to Conform to Settlement to Judgment, moving party was to serve opposing parties by December 24, 2012 at 4:00 p.m. By order dated December 24, 2012, granting TRO and Order to Show Cause, moving party was to serve opposing parties by personal service by December 24, 2012 at 4:00 p.m. These service requirements do not appear to have been met. See "Amended Notice of Order to Show Cause" filed January 11, 2013, attaching a proof of personal service dated December 27, 2012. If there are proofs of service on December 24, 2012, moving party must bring them to the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: CHARLES E. YEAGER et al VS. WILD, CARTER & TIPTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Oder Compelling Pltf'S To Permit Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 2, DEFENDANT DE LA PENA & MCDONALD, LLP'S Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff's To Permit Inspection Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: MARJORIE GEFNER, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HER VS. PEARL PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 3, PLAINTIFF MARJORIE GEFNER, INDIVIDUALLY AND IN HER Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is granted except that the court will not conduct an in camera inspection of documents. Production shall be limited to documents dated subsequent to January 1, 2007. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT HARTMAN VS. JULIANA DROUS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Requiring Pltf To Furnish Security And For Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant From Filing New Litigation In Propria Persona
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT JULIANA DROUS Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Requiring Pltf To Furnish Security And For Prefiling Order Prohibiting Vexatious Litigant From Filing New Litigation In Propria Persona Granted. No opposition filed. The Court finds pursuant to Civil Procedure Code section 391 et seq., including § 391.1, that plaintiff is a vexatious litigant and that there is not a reasonable probability that he will prevail in the litigation against the moving defendant. Plaintiff is ordered to post security in the amount of $3000 within 20 days of this order. A pre-filing order will issue prohibiting Plaintiff Hartman from filing any new litigation in the Courts of this state in propri persona without first obtaining leave of the presiding justice or presiding judge, pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 391.7. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: MALIK MANZOOR AHMED, DBA MANZOOR INTERNATIONAL VS. TAGHI RESAIAN, INDIVIDUALLY, AND DBA SAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT ALI REZAIAN DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead facts showing why Business and Professions Code section 17918 does not apply. Sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to plead facts showing that the first cause of action is not barred by the applicable statute of limitations. Overruled on all other grounds. It cannot be determined from the face of the First Amended Complaint that the claims are barred by the statute of limitations or the statue of frauds or that Plaintiff lacks standing. The allegations are sufficiently certain and Defendant raises factual issues that cannot be determined on demurrer. rulings.Case Title: SHELLEY KRAMER VS. KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK & GONZO LLP, A LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Deft'S Req For Production Of Docs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, January 15, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT KATHERINE CATLOS, AN INDIVIDUAL, KAUFMAN DOLOWICH VOLUCK GONZO, LLP'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant's Request For Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/JPT
